provenance.source_corpus: Smoke and Ash Effects on Seedling Emergence from Germinable Soil Seed Bank in Fescue Prairie

source_spans.text: Smoke plus ash significantly increased the number of Artemisia ludoviciana Nutt. seedlings emerging from 0- to 1-cm soil layer
while ash significantly increased the number of Artemisia frigida Willd. seedlings emerging from 0- to 1-cm soil layer
Smoke plus ash significantly increased the number of Artemisia ludoviciana Nutt. seedlings emerging from 0- to 1-cm soil layer
Smoke plus ash significantly increased the number of Artemisia ludoviciana Nutt. seedlings emerging from 0- to 1-cm soil layer
Conyza canadensis (L.) Cronquist seedlings emerging from the litter layer
ash significantly increased the number of Artemisia frigida Willd. seedlings emerging from 0- to 1-cm soil layer
Total densities of seedlings emerging from the 0- to 1-cm soil layer and all layers combined were significantly increased by smoke plus ash in 2013 and by ash in 2014
Species richness of total seedlings emerging from all soil layers in the soil seed bank was significantly increased by smoke plus ash as compared with the control
Species richness of native species was enhanced by smoke plus ash in all soil layers as compared with the control
Species richness of forbs was significantly increased in all soil layers by smoke plus ash
Smoke significantly altered species composition as compared with the control in the litter layer in 2013
Smoke plus ash increased the rate of emergence of forbs and native species in the litter, 0- to 5-cm, and all layers combined
